title: Greatest Common Divisor

content.problemStatement:
Given two positive integers `A` and `B`, your task is to calculate and print their greatest common divisor (GCD).

The **Greatest Common Divisor (GCD)** of two numbers is the largest positive integer that divides both numbers without leaving a remainder using recursion.


Input Format

A single line containing two space separated integers `A` and `B`.


Output Format

Print a single integer: the GCD of `A` and `B`
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two space-separated positive integers A and B.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the greatest common divisor of A and B.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: gcd(3,18)=3. Test case 2: gcd(1,10)=1. Test case 3: gcd(12,15)=3.
Only 1 divides both 7 and 13, so the GCD is 1.

solution.approach: The Euclidean algorithm computes the GCD of two numbers efficiently. Repeatedly replace the larger number by the remainder of dividing it by the smaller one until the remainder becomes zero; the last non‑zero divisor is the GCD. This works because gcd(a,b)=gcd(b,a mod b). The algorithm runs in O(log min(A,B)) time and uses O(1) extra space. For each test case we apply this algorithm independently.
solution.timeComplexity: O(log min(A,B)) per test case, O(T·log max(A,B)) total

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{private static int gcd(int a,int b){while(b!=0){int t=a%b;a=b;b=t;}return a;}public static void main(String[] args)throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();String line=br.readLine();if(line==null||line.isEmpty())return;int T=Integer.parseInt(line.trim());for(int i=0;i<T;i++){StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());int A=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());int B=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());sb.append(gcd(A,B));if(i+1<T)sb.append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
